title: Business and economics
explanation:
Business and economics

  • revenue, profit, merger, recession, inflation
  • shareholder, investment, startup, bankruptcy, turnover
  • verbs: invest, expand, compete, forecast

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Rising ____ has increased the cost of everyday goods. | *inflation | pollution | humidity
The two companies announced a ____ worth billions. | *merger | holiday | recipe
During a ____, many businesses close or cut jobs. | *recession | celebration | vacation
Annual ____ rose after the successful product launch. | *revenue | luggage | weather

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: They decided to ____ in renewable technology. | invest
Small ____ often struggle to access bank loans. | startups
The firm warned it might face ____ without new funding. | bankruptcy
____ want higher returns on their shares. | Shareholders
Analysts ____ slower growth next year. | forecast

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Net ____ increased despite higher production costs. | *profit | debt | traffic
The shop's annual ____ exceeded expectations. | *turnover | sentence | verdict
Local firms must ____ with cheaper imports. | *compete | apologise | testify
They plan to ____ into three new European markets. | *expand | recycle | interrupt
